raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need to split a tar.gz file into chunks of <=10 MiB. Use split -b 10M? But need to preserve file? We can use split -b 10M -d -a 4 --additional-suffix=.gz? Actually splitting a compressed file into parts. Use split -b 10M /workspace/archive.tar.gz output_prefix. That will create files output_prefixaa, etc. But maybe want to keep .gz? Use --additional-suffix=.gz. So command: split -b 10M /workspace/archive.tar.gz output_prefix. That's fine.
